quizQuestion:
Es necesario que {___} todos presentes.
(It's necessary that we all be present.)
sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that we all be present.
explanation: estemos — present subjunctive of estar (nosotros). Es necesario que triggers subjunctive. Paradigm: esté, estés, esté, estemos, estéis, estén.

quizQuestion:
Quiero que {___} contentos.
(I want them to be happy.)
sentenceTranslation: I want them to be happy.
explanation: estén — present subjunctive of estar (ellos/ustedes). Quiero que requires subjunctive. estar subjunctive: esté, estés, esté, estemos, estéis, estén.

quizQuestion:
{___} solo, señor.
(Don't go alone, sir.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't go alone, sir.
grammarPointLabel: negative usted imperative: ir → no vaya
explanation: no vaya — negative usted imperative of ir. Uses subjunctive: vaya (usted subjunctive of ir). Affirmative: vaya usted. Negative: no vaya usted. The vaya form serves both purposes.

quizQuestion:
{___} ruido, por favor.
(Please don't make noise.)
sentenceTranslation: Please don't make noise.
grammarPointLabel: negative ustedes imperative: hacer → no hagan
explanation: no hagan — negative ustedes imperative of hacer. Uses subjunctive: hagan (ustedes). No hagan ruido = please don't make noise. Common in public spaces (libraries, cinemas).

quizQuestion:
{___} sin paraguas — llueve mucho.
(Don't go out without an umbrella — it's raining a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't go out without an umbrella — it's raining a lot.
grammarPointLabel: negative usted imperative: salir → no salga
explanation: no salga — negative usted imperative of salir. salir subjunctive: salga. Usted: no salga. Contrast with tú: no salgas. The formal register uses -a ending for -ir verbs (salga, no salga).

quizQuestion:
{___} su nombre aquí, por favor.
(Put your name here, please.)
sentenceTranslation: Put your name here, please.
explanation: ponga — affirmative usted imperative of poner. Uses subjunctive: ponga. Tú affirmative: pon vs. usted: ponga. Common in formal instructions and forms.

quizQuestion:
{___} por la puerta de la izquierda.
(Exit through the left door.)
sentenceTranslation: Exit through the left door.
explanation: salga — affirmative usted imperative of salir. Uses subjunctive: salga. Tú affirmative: sal vs. usted: salga. Common in formal directions and signage.

quizQuestion:
{___} al médico mañana.
(Go to the doctor tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: Go to the doctor tomorrow.
explanation: vaya — affirmative usted imperative of ir (irregular). ir subjunctive: vaya. Tú affirmative: ve vs. usted: vaya. Vaya is also used as an exclamation (Vaya! = Well!/What a...!).

quizQuestion:
{___} el ejercicio número tres.
(Do exercise number three.)
sentenceTranslation: Do exercise number three.
explanation: hagan — affirmative ustedes imperative of hacer. Uses subjunctive (hagan). Common in classroom instructions. Contrast: usted → haga / ustedes → hagan.

quizQuestion:
{___} cuidado con el tráfico.
(Be careful with traffic.)
sentenceTranslation: Be careful with traffic.
explanation: tengan — affirmative ustedes imperative of tener. Uses subjunctive (tengan). Tengan cuidado is the formal/plural equivalent of Ten cuidado (tú) / Tenga cuidado (usted).

quizQuestion:
{___} directamente al aeropuerto.
(Go directly to the airport.)
sentenceTranslation: Go directly to the airport.
explanation: vayan — affirmative ustedes imperative of ir. Uses subjunctive (vayan). ir imperative across persons: ve (tú), vaya (usted), vayamos (nosotros), vayan (ustedes).

quizQuestion:
{___} optimistas.
(Let's be optimistic.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's be optimistic.
explanation: seamos — nosotros imperative of ser. Uses subjunctive (seamos). Let's be = seamos. The irregular subjunctive form of ser also serves as the nosotros imperative.

quizQuestion:
{___} paciencia.
(Let's be patient.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's be patient.
explanation: tengamos — nosotros imperative of tener. Uses subjunctive (tengamos). tener subjunctive nosotros: tengamos. Tengamos paciencia = let's be patient / let's have patience.

quizQuestion:
{___} un descanso.
(Let's take a break.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's take a break.
explanation: hagamos — nosotros imperative of hacer. Uses subjunctive (hagamos). Hagamos un descanso = let's take a break. hacer nosotros subjunctive: hagamos.

quizQuestion:
{___} más si tuviera tiempo.
(I would study more if I had time.)
sentenceTranslation: I would study more if I had time.
explanation: estudiaría — conditional of estudiar (yo). estudiar + ía = estudiaría. Regular: any -ar/-er/-ir verb: add -ía to full infinitive. This constructs a hypothetical situation.

quizQuestion:
Con más dinero, {___} menos horas.
(With more money, they would work fewer hours.)
sentenceTranslation: With more money, they would work fewer hours.
explanation: trabajarían — conditional of trabajar (ellos). trabajar + ían = trabajarían. Ellos conditional ends in -ían (with accent). Expresses a hypothetical result.

quizQuestion:
Mañana {___} con el director.
(Tomorrow they will speak with the director.)
sentenceTranslation: Tomorrow they will speak with the director.
explanation: hablarán — future of hablar (ellos/ellas). hablar + án = hablarán. ellos future ends in -án (with accent). Full paradigm: hablaré, hablarás, hablará, hablaremos, hablaréis, hablarán.

quizQuestion:
Si consigues el trabajo, {___} mucho.
(If you get the job, you'll work a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: If you get the job, you'll work a lot.
explanation: trabajarás — future of trabajar (tú). trabajar + ás = trabajarás. This is also a si-clause type 1: si consigues (present) + trabajarás (future).

quizQuestion:
{___} tienes tiempo, llámame.
(If you have time, call me.)
sentenceTranslation: If you have time, call me.
explanation: si — the conjunction si (if) introduces type 1 conditional clauses. Structure: si + present indicative + present/future/imperative. Si tienes tiempo (present indicative in condition), llámame (imperative in result). Si NEVER takes subjunctive in type 1.

quizQuestion:
{___} comes bien, te sentirás mejor.
(If you eat well, you'll feel better.)
sentenceTranslation: If you eat well, you'll feel better.
explanation: si — Si comes bien (condition: present indicative) → te sentirás mejor (result: future). Type 1 si-clauses express real, likely conditions. Compare: cuando comes bien, te sientes mejor (habitual).

quizQuestion:
{___} no estudias, suspenderás.
(If you don't study, you'll fail.)
sentenceTranslation: If you don't study, you'll fail.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 1: negative condition → negative result
explanation: si — negative type 1 si-clause. Si no estudias (negative present) → suspenderás (future). The si-clause can come first or second: Suspenderás si no estudias. Contrast: si no estudiases, suspenderías (type 2, B2+).

quizQuestion:
{___} me llamas, te ayudaré.
(If you call me, I'll help you.)
sentenceTranslation: If you call me, I'll help you.
explanation: si — Si me llamas (condition: present indicative) → te ayudaré (result: future). The future tense in the result clause is characteristic of type 1 si-clauses. Key contrast: si + present (type 1) vs. si + imperfect subjunctive (type 2, B2).

quizQuestion:
Está {___} ahora.
(He's explaining it to me now.)
sentenceTranslation: He's explaining it to me now.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to gerund → explicándomelo
explanation: explicándomelo — explicando + me + lo = explicándomelo. When pronouns attach to gerunds, add an accent on the stressed syllable: explicÁNdomelo. IO (me) before DO (lo). Alternative: me lo está explicando.

quizQuestion:
Siguió {___}.
(He kept telling it to me.)
sentenceTranslation: He kept telling it to me.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to gerund → diciéndomelo
explanation: diciéndomelo — diciendo + me + lo = diciéndomelo. decir gerund: diciendo (stem change e→i). With attached pronouns: diciÉNdomelo. Used with seguir + gerundio periphrasis.

quizQuestion:
Voy a {___}.
(I'm going to explain it to you.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm going to explain it to you.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to infinitive → explicártelo
explanation: explicártelo — explicar + te + lo = explicártelo. With ir a + infinitive, pronouns can attach to the infinitive or go before ir: Voy a explicártelo = Te lo voy a explicar. Accent on verb: explicÁRtelo.

quizQuestion:
{___}.
(Tell it to me.)
sentenceTranslation: Tell it to me.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to usted affirmative imperative → dígamelo
explanation: dígamelo — diga (usted imperative of decir) + me + lo = dígamelo. Accent on diga → dÍgamelo. Usted imperatives attach pronouns: dígame (tell me), déselo (give it to him/her), dígamelo (tell it to me).
